description: An outbreak of serious illness associated with eating foraged wild mushrooms has been reported. CDPH is urging individuals to avoid foraging for and eating wild mushrooms while risk of amatoxin poisoning is high.

title: Park Headquarters Road closed
description: Park Headquarters Road is closed due to rehabilitation work on the Sugarlands water and wastewater systems.

title: Limited access to some trails due to washouts from winter storms.
description: The following trails have been impacted by winter storms: Carter Falls Trail footbridge over the Nisqually River has been dislodged and there is no safe water crossing; Kautz Creek Trail is impassable one mile from the trailhead due to a washout. There may be other trails with unreported storm impacts around the park.

title: Homestead Closure
description: The historic Andrew Johnson Homestead is closed. All tours and access to this structure are cancelled until further notice.

title: South Parking Lot of Greenbelt Park Closed to Vehicles until March 31, 2026
description: The south parking lot of Greenbelt Park will be closed to vehicle access until March 31, 2026, at 11:59 p.m. The area will remain available to pedestrians.

title: Greenbelt Park Perimeter Trail Closed until March 31, 2026
description: The section of the perimeter trail from the 0.8 mile marker to the 1.9 mile marker at Park Central Road is closed for repairs until March 31, 2026, at 11:59 p.m.

title: Annual closures to better protect elephant seal pups will be in effect from Dec. 15 until March 31.
description: Annual beach closures for the elephant seal breeding season start December 15 and go through March 31. Closures include the southern end of South Beach, the beach from the Lifeboat Station to Chimney Rock, the Fish Dock area, and portions of Drakes Beach. Please stay out of closed areas for your safety and the safety of the seals.

title: Staple Bend Tunnel Closure
description: Due to a rockfall, the interior of the Staple Bend Tunnel is closed. The trail is still open. From the Mineral Point Trailhead, visitors can bypass the tunnel, via a path around the right side of the tunnel structure.

title: Pierce Stocking Scenic Drive closed for annual winter/spring season
description: The Pierce Stocking Scenic Drive is closed to vehicles for the annual winter and spring closure. The drive is open for hiking and walk-in recreation during the vehicle closure.
relatedRoadEvents: [{'title': 'Pierce Stocking Scenic Drive annual winter/spring road closure', 'id': 'B497521F-816F-4622-9333-7EAAC75D0CA8', 'type': 'roadevent', 'url': 'https://www.nps.gov/slbe/planyourvisit/conditions.htm'}]

title: Ride Share Services - pickup limited
description: Taxi and ride-share services are often not available in Pecos. Even if services like Uber and Lyft will bring you here, it can be very difficult to secure a ride out. Please ensure you have a departure plan in place before being dropped off at Pecos.

title: Accessibility Lifts at Fort Sumter Inoperable Until Further Notice
description: The accessibility lifts at Fort Sumter are currently out of order. The only way to access level 2 and level 3 of the Fort is via a staircase until further notice.

title: Temporary Pause on Research and Donation Requests
description: Please be advised that due to staffing changes and the ongoing hiring freeze, there is a pause on research requests and donation considerations at all parks in the Manhattan Sites Unit until further notice. Please check this website and social media for updates.

title: Temporary Pause on Research and Donation Requests
description: Please be advised that due to staffing changes and the ongoing hiring freeze, there is a pause on research requests and donation considerations at all parks in the Manhattan Sites Unit until further notice. Please check this website and social media for updates.

title: Sand Board and Sand Sled Rentals Not Available In Park.
description: Sand boarding and sand sled rentals are located in local communities surrounding the park. Visitors are encouraged to arrange rentals BEFORE coming to the park.

title: Parking Reservations Always Required
description: Reservations are required for all personal vehicles and shuttle riders visiting Muir Woods. You can make your reservation by visiting www.gomuirwoods.com. Visit our "Know Before You Go" webpage to learn more important details before your visit.

title: No Cell Service at Muir Woods
description: There is NO cell service at Muir Woods. We discourage scheduling a rideshare service to leave the park. When hiring a taxi, visitors have the option of negotiating a flat rate or using the meter. Under some circumstances a flat rate may be cheaper.

title: Park Wide Caution
description: Visitors should exercise caution when hiking areas of the park. Hazards on trails and in forested areas such as hanging tree limbs, debris on the ground, and washed-out sections of trails and bridges are possible.

title: Park Wide Caution
description: Visitors should exercise caution when hiking areas of the park that have opened since Hurricane Helene. Hazards on trails and in forested areas such as hanging tree limbs, debris on the ground, and washed-out sections of trails and bridges are possible.

title: Phone Lines Down
description: Our phone lines are being upgraded and are currently down. If you need to contact us please email: NISI_Information@nps.gov Or you may call Cowpens National Battlefield at: 864 461-2828

title: Cave Tour Reservations
description: Advance cave tour reservations up until April 30th are available. Book ahead. Reservations full? Choose a day to come early to the park as close to opening as possible to purchase a same day cave tour ticket at the park.

title: We cannot mail out at this time
description: We do not mail Junior Ranger books; please download from site. Completed books may be submitted digitally. Junior Ranger badges may be mailed once per person per calendar year after completion. We do not mail completed books back.

title: We cannot mail out at this time
description: We do not mail Junior Ranger books; please download from site. Completed books may be submitted digitally. Junior Ranger badges may be mailed once per person per calendar year after completion. We do not mail completed books back.

title: Winter Hours
description: Fort Stanwix Visitor Center/Fort are now closed for the winter season but park grounds will remain open and accessible. Park facilities anticipated reopening in mid April. Please check the park website for reopening updates come spring.

title: Routine Winter Service Levels in Effect
description: Roads and areas of the park may be closed or inaccessible during the winter, Please check the park's current conditions page to learn more.

title: America the Beautiful Pass not Issued at Wilson's Creek
description: Wilson's Creek National Battlefield is a fee free site. No pass or charge is required to visit. Due to being a fee free site, America the Beautiful Passes are no longer issued. To learn about different pass types and where you can obtain them, including new digital passes, please visit the provided link.

title: Washington Monument is closed for maintenance one day each month
description: Closures for 2025: Dec 11, 25. Closures for 2026: Jan 8, Feb 3, Mar 4, Apr 6, May 7, Jun 2, July 4, July 8, Aug 3, Sep 10, Oct 5, Nov 11,26, Dec 25. The monument is also closed for the holidays on July 4 and Dec 25 each year.

title: Winter Weather Information
description: Park grounds remain open in snowy or icy weather, however parking areas are not cleared until 1-3 days after a snowfall ends. Trails and parking lot may be icy or uncleared. Use caution if driving to the park and onsite as conditions may be hazardous.

title: Eastern Front Tour Road south of Route 36 - Closed
description: The Eastern Front Tour Road south of Route 36 is closed to all vehicles due to a sinkhole near Harrison Creek (stop 4). Walkers and bicyclist are allowed to use the road. Use caution in the Harrison Creek area.
relatedRoadEvents: [{'title': 'Eastern Front Tour Road south of Route 36 - Closed', 'id': 'FF05DA0E-813F-450F-880A-A818832377D3', 'type': 'roadevent', 'url': 'https://www.nps.gov/pete/planyourvisit/conditions.htm'}]

title: Island Ford Trail Closures Winter 2025-2026
description: All trails at the Island Ford Unit of CRNRA will be closed December 8 - 11. Crews will be felling hazardous trees; for visitor safety and project efficiency please stay off the trails. The Visitor Center remains open 9am - 5pm. Trails will be open until January 5. From January 5 until Spring 2026, Island Ford trails will be closed as we improve the trail system and add 2.2 miles of new trails. For more information, visit chattahoocheeparks.org/iftrails.

title: Acadian Cultural Center Temporarily Closed
description: The Acadian Cultural Center in Lafayette is temporarily closed due to HVAC issues. Join us at the Prairie Acadian Cultural Center in Eunice for programs and events 5 days a week, Tuesday through Saturday.

title: Thomas Stone National Historic Site Closed for Winter
description: Thomas Stone National Historic Site will reopen in Spring 2026.

title: Winter Ice and Snow Conditions
description: Please use extra caution when visiting the park during winter. Park staff work hard to ensure that parking areas and sidewalks are treated as quickly as possible, however most of the park grounds and trails are not treated during the winter months. So, be careful out there and remember to dial 911 in the event of an emergency. Park staff will be notified by emergency dispatch.

title: Reef Ride Bike Days 2026
description: For this special event in honor of National Public Lands Day, the Scenic Drive will be closed to motor vehicles for part of the day on Saturday, September 19 and Saturday, September 26, 2026. The road will be closed to vehicles from sunrise to 3 pm to allow for bicycles and pedestrians to enjoy the road at a more leisurely pace. The main road through the park (UT highway 24) - will remain open and unaffected by this closure.

title: Park Visitor Center Is Closed
description: Due to damage from the 2024 Hurricanes. The Park's visitor centers and restrooms are closed due to health and safety issues. The Park has installed portable restrooms located at the front of the park for visitor use.

title: Visitor Center and Bookstore Open Wed - Sun
description: Visitor Center and Bookstore hours are Wed - Sun 10-4. It is .5 mile walk to the historic area from the Hikers Lot. Please keep this in mind. If you have access needs, please call the number posted in the parking lot.

title: Cascade River Road Winter Closure
description: Cascade River Road is closed to vehicles at milepost 21 (Johnson Cabin) two miles before the trailhead. Foot and bicycle traffic is permitted beyond the gate.

title: Winter Hours
description: December 1, 2025, through March 7, 2026, (change to Daylight Savings Time) all sites are open from 10am to 4pm EST.

title: Most Park Roads Not Maintained for Winter
description: Sand Point Road is the only park road maintained in winter. Miners Castle Road, Chapel Road, Beaver Road, and H-58 between Grand Marais and Adam's Trail are not maintained.

title: Winter Hours: 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM
description: From Labor Day through Memorial Day, the Birthplace will be open from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM. The tour schedule remains the same.

title: Fish cleaning station at Flamingo is temporarily closed
description: The fish cleaning station at the Flamingo marina is closed from Dec. 1 for a few weeks, while we await a new part for the grinder.

title: No Potable Water in Park Campgrounds
description: There currently is no water available in any campground in Great Basin National Park.  Water is available at some local businesses.

title: SR 165 Carbon River/Fairfax Bridge is closed: no access to Carbon River or Mowich Lake.
description: There is NO public access to Carbon River & Mowich Lake from SR 165. The bridge is closed to pedestrians, bicycles and vehicles and there is no alternate route.

title: Guided Tours at the Reconstructed Fort
description: Regularly scheduled guided tours provide access into furnished rooms and explore the fort’s role in Westward Expansion and the reconstruction of the fort in 1976.

title: Hart Mine Road Closed
description: Hart Mine Road Closed.

title: NPS Visitor Wifi
description: Visitor wifi and wifi calling is now available in The Kelso Depot parking lot for calls including emergencies. Go to the bulletin board at the Kelso Depot restrooms for instructions.

title: Increased Logging Activity
description: Logging trucks and other large machinery will be active on portions of Swift Brook and Roberts Road for an undetermined amount of time. Please use caution when driving. Always pull over for logging trucks and only continue driving when it is deemed safe.

title: EV Charging Stations Unavailable
description: Electric Vehicle (EV) Charging stations are currently unavailable in the parking facility due to an ongoing construction project.

title: Capacity Restrictions at the Reconstructed Fort
description: The design of the reconstructed fort fails to meet accessibility, fire, and life-safety requirements of 1976. A lack of code compliant components such as emergency lighting, illuminated exit signage, panic hardware on doors, and outwardly swinging exit doors results in a limitation of 49 occupants for visitor operations within the reconstructed fort.

title: Plaza Access to Reconstructed Fort
description: Entry into the reconstructed fort is limited to the central plaza on scheduled days. For your safety respect all closures and restrictions. Guided tours provide access into additional areas.

title: Visitor Services Available Only at the Site
description: All visitor Services are available at the Historic Site, 23 miles northeast of Eads, Colorado. The Center for Sand Creek Massacre Studies located in Eads, Colorado is available for appointment only research requests.

title: 8 Miles of Unpaved Road to Reach Park
description: In order to reach the Sand Creek Massacre Site, visitors must travel on at least 8 miles of unpaved roads. We are not able to guarantee road conditions of county roads 54 or W after spring storms.

title: A Short Walk is Required to Reach the Fort
description: A 1/4 mile (1,275 feet) walk on a paved trail from the parking area is required to reach the gate of the fort. At the end of your visit, you'll return to your vehicle along the same path.

title: Visitor Centers are Closed for the Season
description: All park visitor centers have closed for the season. This includes the Houghton, Rock Harbor, & Windigo/Ozaagaateng Visitor Centers.

title: Isle Royale National Park to Reopen April 16, 2026
description: From November 1 - April 15 annually, Isle Royale and its surrounding islands are closed to all visitors. Lake Superior waters are open to navigation. All islands reopen April 16. Call (906) 482-0984 to discuss trip planning, obtain permits, or pay fees.

title: Seasonal Operations
description: Governors Island National Monument will be closed this winter. Both Castle Williams and Fort Jay will be closed and access to NPS staff will be extremely limited. The rest of the island will be open for visitors. Check https://www.govisland.com/ for island winter events and ferry schedule. Look to this page for re-opening information in the spring of 2026. Use our contact link for questions and requests. Questions or requests should be directed to our contact link on this website. Thank you!

title: Springhill Barn: repairs and maintenance
description: The historic Spring Hill Barn is closed until further notice for repairs and maintenance.

title: Parking at Boundary Access Areas
description: Visitors are reminded to park in designated parking areas when accessing the park via boundary trailheads. Parking in unauthorized locations may result in your vehicle being towed at the owner's expense. Park on the right side of the road approaching Thompson Hollow. Parking is authorized up to the "No Parking" sign before a private driveway.

title: Trail closure for pipeline restoration project
description: The VC09/South Valle Toledo/Posos Trail from the Valle Toledo parking area to the eastern park boundary is closed for restoration. The NPS is working on a trail reroute and will provide details as soon as possible.

title: Backcountry Trail Conditions
description: GPS is necessary on backcountry trails: Oakridge, Boggy Gut (formerly River), and Kingsnake. These trails may be difficult to follow.

title: Kelly Point Road Winter Motorized Vehicle Restriction Begins November 21st.
description: Motorized vehicles are not allowed on Kelly Point Road in winter (NPS1203) without a permit. This restriction is in effect beginning Friday, November 21st, 2025.

title: Water in campground is shut off for the winter
description: Due to overnight freezing temperatures, water in the campground has been shut off for the season. Visitors should plan to bring their own water. Small containers may be filled at the visitor center during business hours.

title: Washington Monument closed one day each month for maintenance
description: Closures for 2025: Dec 11, 25. Closures for 2026: Jan 8, Feb 3, Mar 4, Apr 6, May 7, Jun 2, July 8, Aug 3, Sep 10, Oct 5, Nov 11,26, Dec 25. The monument is also closed for the Holidays on July 4 and Dec 25 each year.

title: Site Closure Beginning July 8, 2024
description: The Edgar Allan Poe National Historic Site remains closed for a utilities improvement project. Check back in 2026 for a reopening date.

title: Delta-01 Tours Not Available at this Time
description: Minuteman Missile National Historic Site is unable to offer ranger-guided Delta-01 tours at this time. Existing tour reservations will be honored. An alternative virtual tour of the Delta-01 Launch Control Facility is available online or at the visitor center as staffing allows.

title: Volcano Top Access Closed to Vehicles over 26 feet long and to all Trailers
description: The Volcano top access is closed to all RVs, buses, attached pull trailers, or any other vehicle over 26 feet in length due to public safety concerns and limited parking.  Trailer drop-off and RV/bus parking are available at the visitor center.

title: Fire Restrictions
description: Fire restrictions have been lifted at Carlsbad Caverns which means that fires such as charcoal fires in grills and the use of camp stoves or portable grills will be allowed. Igniting and maintaining an open fire must be done with materials that are commonly used for warmth and food preparations such as natural firewood or charcoal briquettes. Other materials such as flammable liquids, garbage, plastic, aerosol canisters, or other artificial substances are not permitted.

title: Winter Hours of Operation
description: Effective immediately, Martin Van Buren National Historic Site is in winter hours of operation. Both Lindenwald (the historic home of President Martin Van Buren) and the park visitor center are closed to the public. Visitor operations, including guided house tours and visitor center services, are anticipated to resume at a TBD date in May/June 2026. The park grounds remain open daily from 7:00 a.m. until dusk. Please note that no visitor services are available during this period.

title: Winter Season Closures
description: The following regular winter season road closures are in place: Westside Road, Paradise Valley Road, Stevens Canyon Road, White River Road, Sunrise Road, State Routes 410/Chinook Pass (between Crystal Mountain Blvd. & Morse Creek) and 123/Cayuse Pass (at the park boundary).

title: Sections of moat wall closed until Summer 2026
description: Sections of the moat wall that were damaged by Hurricanes Irma (2017) and Ian (2022), as well as several hurricanes in 2024 are undergoing repair and reinforcement. For your safety, please follow posted signage and do not cross any barricades.

title: Chatham Closed for Rehabilitation Project
description: Chatham's interior will be closed until at least summer 2026 for a major rehabilitation project. The grounds remain open sunrise to sunset, and the gated parking area will be open from 9-5 daily.

title: Beach Access Ramp Closure
description: Beginning November 17, the Fort Matanzas beach ramp will be closed to the public for ramp and parking lot improvements as well as the construction of a new public access boardwalk to the beach. The NPS anticipates this construction will take place through May of 2026. During this time no public access by FOOT or VEHICLE is allowed. We appreciate your patience and understanding as we work to improve Fort Matanzas National Monument. This closure does NOT affect Ferry Boat operations to Fort Matanzas.

title: All lava tube caves closed November 1 through April 30
description: All park lava tube caves are closed for the winter from November 1 through April 30 to protect hibernating bats.

title: Closed Tuesdays and Wednesdays
description: Winter hours are in effect: the visitor center and park trails are open five days a week, from Thursday to Monday. Closed on Tuesdays and Wednesdays. There is no trail access when the visitor center is closed.

title: Park Unable to Receive Phone Messages
description: At this time, phone messages cannot be left at the visitor center. Please email the park at moca_tuzi_info@nps.gov or call during operating hours if you need to contact the park.

title: Winter Visitor Center Hours
description: The Visitor Center is currently open five days a week, Wednesday to Sunday, from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. See Operating Hours and Seasons for more information.

title: Visitor Center Closed for the Winter Season
description: The visitor center is now closed for the season. Due to limited staffing, we are unable to offer tours till we reopen in the summer. For more information, please call 530-667-8119 or email us at tule_information@nps.gov.

title: Park Information Center - Temporarily Moved
description: Please Note: The Park Visitor Center at 450 Auburn Avenue is closed for renovations. For Park information visit Park Rangers at Fire Station No. 6 (39 Boulevard N.E. Atlanta, Georgia) Monday - Sunday, 9:00 am to 5:00 pm. Visitor parking GPS: 423 John Wesley Dobbs NE Atlanta Georgia. View the Park calendar for daily Ranger led tours & talks.

title: Loop Road and North Entrance Seasonal Closures
description: The Loop Road and North Entrance are closed to motorized vehicles until May 2026. These areas are accessible by foot, bike, ski, and snowshoe. Restrooms and campsites are accessible but not maintained in the off-season. Learn more about winter recreation and seasonal closures below.

title: Katahdin Overlook Construction Closure
description: The Katahdin Overlook is currently closed for construction. Visitors are not permitted in the overlook area due to construction hazards. All other locations on the Loop Road are accessible by motor vehicles seasonally and by foot year round.

title: Winter Weather Notifications - Please call ahead if the weather is bad
description: Winter weather in the Allegheny Mountains can cause delays in the park opening or closing, as roads and lots are cleared. Call 724-329-5802 for the latest information on park closures and delays. Just because the weather is nice where you are, doesn't mean it will remain so on the mountain. If the park has a delayed opening, please give us time to clear parking lots, roads and trails before visiting. Some areas within the park do not receive winter maintenance, and cell phone service can be spotty.

title: Seasonal Closures
description: Jumonville Glen, Mount Washington Tavern and the Picnic Area have closed for the season. They will reopen on May 1, 2026!

title: Big Savage Tunnel To Close for Winter
description: Somerset County will close the Big Savage Tunnel for the 2025-26 winter season on Wednesday, November 26. This seasonal closure, performed annually, protects the lining of the tunnel from freeze-thaw cycles brought on by high winds, cold temperatures, and wet, snowy conditions at high elevation. Please note that there is no detour, and local roads are narrow and treacherous. Please plan your winter excursions elsewhere on the GAP. The tunnel will reopen in late March or early April, depending on weather.

title: Winter Hours Begin on October 1, 2025: 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM
description: Little Bighorn Battlefield will be moving to our winter hours beginning October 1st. All available facilities will be open at 8:00 AM. The Tour Road, Last Stand Hill, and Reno-Benteen area will close at 3:30 PM. The contact station, bookstore, and national cemetery will close at 4:00 PM.

title: Oversized Vehicles are Unable to Park in Visitor Center Parking Lot
description: Due to construction, oversized vehicles will not be allowed to park in the visitor center parking lot due to lack of space and ability to turn-around safely. There is oversized parking along the main park road.

title: The Park is ONLY OPEN Friday, Saturday, and Sunday.
description: The park is closed Monday through Thursday. Visitors can expect this closure to last through the foreseeable future. There is no access to any part of the park during closures. For questions call 406-924-9167.

title: Medano Pass Winter Season Closures in Effect
description: The Medano Pass Primitive Road is closed to through traffic for the winter season.

title: Weir House, Weir Studio, Young Studio, Visitor Center, and Restrooms Closed
description: Please note all park buildings are closed for the season through April. Park grounds are open daily year-round and maps, brochures, and passport stamp are available in the parking lot kiosk. Restrooms are not available during the off-season.

title: Visitor Center Open at 419 Decatur Street
description: The New Orleans Jazz NHP & Jean Lafitte NHP&P have a combined French Quarter Visitor Center at 419 Decatur Street. Open Tuesdays through Saturdays, 9:30 am to 4:30 pm. Calendar for events: https://www.nps.gov/jazz/planyourvisit/calendar.

title: Thaddeus Kosciuszko National Memorial Closed for the Season
description: Closed for the season. The site will reopen in Spring of 2026. Usual site hours: Saturday & Sunday, 12–4 pm.

title: The Picnic Area, Burial Ground, and Potomac River Beach are open Wednesday through Sunday
description: The Picnic Area, Washington Family Burial Ground, and Potomac River Beach are open Wednesday through Sunday, 9:00 am to 5:00 pm

title: The Visitor Center is open Wednesday - Sunday from 9:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.
description: The Visitor Center is open Wednesday - Sunday from 9:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.

title: Gallatin House Hours Changing
description: From November 14th to December 13th, the Gallatin House and Visitor Center will be open Fridays to Mondays, 9 AM to 5 PM (CLOSED Tuesdays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays). On Saturday, December 13th, the Gallatin House and Visitor Center will only be open Saturdays and Sundays until further notice. The grounds and trails will be available from sunrise to sunset year round.

title: Winter Closure Information Line
description: Winter weather in southwestern Pennsylvania change often and can cause delays in park opening as roads, walks, and parking areas are cleared. Call 724-329-5802 for the latest information on park closures and delays.

title: Moores Creek Visitor Center Open Tuesday-Saturday and Park Grounds Open Seven Days Per Week
description: The Moores Creek Visitor Center/Bookstore is open Tuesday to Saturday from 9 am until 5 pm. Park grounds are open seven days per week from 9 am until 5 pm.

title: New Park Hours
description: As of November 13, 2025, the new hours of operation at Puʻukoholā Heiau National Historic Site are 8:30am –4:30pm. The parking lot gate will close at 4:00pm.

title: Temporary Nighttime Road Closures for Deer Management
description: From November 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026, the NPS may temporarily close select park roads between 5 pm to 4 am to conduct deer management operations. Per the Rock Creek Park Superintendent’s Compendium, trails and other park areas close at sunset.

title: Boquillas Crossing Port of Entry is open Friday-Monday 9:00 am to 4:00 pm
description: Until further notice, the Boquillas Crossing Port of Entry will be open Friday through Monday, 9:00 am through 4:00 pm. It will not change to winter hours at this time.

title: Some Park Buildings are Temporarily Closed for Maintenance
description: Some park buildings are temporarily closed to clean up after the Fire Suppression System replacement. Closed are Officer's Quarters #2 and some of the Dragoon Barracks. A virtual tour link is below.

title: All Lava Tube Caves Closed Due to Hazardous Conditions
description: All lava tube caves are CLOSED due to snow and ice. Entry to caves is prohibited.

title: Temporary Bridge on North Shore Quinault Rd Removed for Winter
description: A temporary bridge on the North Shore Quinault Road was removed for the winter on October 23, 2025. The bridge will be reinstalled next spring as conditions allow. The Graves Creek area cannot be accessed via South Shore Road due to a washout beyond the national park boundary.

title: Winter storms and trail damage
description: With winter windstorms coming and going, tree and trail damage will be fixed as weather and staffing allows. If there is a tree down or trail damage, please report it to the visitor center or to LEWI_information@nps.gov with exact location of the damage.

title: Cliff Dwellings Are Closed During the Winter and Early Spring Seasons
description: Cliff dwelling tours and access to cliff dwellings are closed for the season. Twenty miles (45-minute drive) from the park entrance is the Mesa Verde Museum and Spruce Tree House Overlook. Views of ancestral sites are seen along the Mesa Top Loop Road. Click the link for information about visiting in winter.

title: Credit/Debit Card Only to pay entrance fees
description: Capulin Volcano only accepts credit/debit cards for entrance fees. Our cooperating association Park Store accepts cash, credit/debit cards, and checks. There are limited cash only entrance passes available through our cooperating association Park Store.

title: Reservations Required for All Campgrounds
description: As of January 1, 2025, ALL developed campsites will be available through reservation ONLY. These include Pulltite, Round Spring, Alley Spring, Big Spring, Two Rivers, and Akers. Reservations must be made through recreation.gov (online or by calling).

title: Park Hours may Change due to Winter Storms
description: Opening delays or temporary closures due to blowing snow, accumulation, and icy roads are likely when these conditions exist. Roads will reopen when snowplows have cleared them for safe two-way travel. Before visiting, check this page for up-to-date information. Colorado Traction Law may apply, see info below. Click link for weather.

title: No Water Available in Park
description: Water systems throughout the park have been shut off for the winter season, October to April. Please plan ahead and bring your own drinking water.

title: Intermittent Lane Closures at GW Parkway for Bridge/Pavement Preservation
description: Beginning on Monday, November 17, 2025, expect intermittent traffic impacts at GWMP. This Bridge and pavement preservation work is expected to extend into the Fall of 2026 and will make repairs to 24 GWMP bridges and over 30 paved roads and parking assets. To minimize traffic impacts, weekday lane closures on George Washington Memorial Parkway, Clara Barton Parkway, and Spout Run Parkway will NOT be allowed inbound to DC from 5:30am to 9:30am and outbound from DC from 2:30pm to 7:30pm Monday through Friday.

title: Straight Fork and the one-way section of Balsam Mountain Road closed.
description: Straight Fork and the one-way section of Balsam Mountain Road (Heintooga Round-Bottom Road) are closed.

title: Water Unavailable at Hensley Settlement effective October 22, 2025
description: To prevent freezing of and subsequent damage to pipes, all water at Hensley Settlement has been turned off. Hikers should plan accordingly and carry water in sufficient amounts. Any water collected from streams must be purified.

title: Upper Fort Mason Roads and Parking Area Closures Fall 2025 - Fall 2026
description: Roads and parking areas at upper Fort Mason will have closures during utilities and seismic retrofit projects. Closures planned Fall 2025 through Fall 2026. Lots will be fenced and used as staging areas for projects occurring at Fort Mason. Signs will be posted in advance of utilities work as project progresses through each area at Fort Mason. Read signs and do not park in posted areas. Vehicles will be towed at owner's expense.

title: Winter Seasonal Road Closures
description: Portions of the Teton Park Road (Taggart Lake TH to Signal Mt.), Moose-Wilson Road (Granite Canyon TH to Death Canyon Road), and other roads are closed to vehicles. Check the Grand Teton Roads page for the latest seasonal and weather related closures. When free of snow, Teton Park and Moose-Wilson Roads are available for pedestrian use, including walking and biking. After they are snow-covered: skiing, snowshoeing, and walking are allowed.

title: North Entrance Road and Rim Drive are CLOSED for the Season
description: The North Entrance Road and Rim Drive are now CLOSED for the season. They will not reopen until mid to late June or July 2026.
relatedRoadEvents: [{'title': 'North Entrance Road and Rim Drive are CLOSED for the Season', 'id': '3AB12B2F-7C46-4EFF-BF4B-B431F4FB2C81', 'type': 'roadevent', 'url': 'https://www.nps.gov/crla/planyourvisit/conditions.htm'}]

title: The campground is closed for the season.
description: The campground is closed for the season. The campground will reopen for the season in Mid-May.

title: No Gasoline Available in Park
description: The gas station at Mazama Village has closed for the season. No gasoline is available for purchase in the park. Running out of gas will require calling a tow truck to assist. Please plan accordingly!

title: Carter G. Woodson Home NHS temporarily closed.
description: The Carter G. Woodson Home National Historic Site is temporarily closed due to a full renovation project. We hope to reopen to the public in 2026. Please continue to visit the website for future updates.

title: National Cemetery Closed to Vehicles; Open to Pedestrians
description: Due to weather related incidents, Vicksburg National Cemetery is closed to vehicular traffic, however it is open to pedestrians. Visitor can park in the adjacent USS Cairo parking lot and walk into the cemetery.

title: Louisiana Circle Closed
description: Louisiana Circle is closed to vehicles and pedestrians due to slope instability. Navy Circle is open and also provides views of the Mississippi River.

title: Seasonal Closure: Gap Cave & Skylight Cave
description: Gap and Skylight Caves are closed for the season; access and tours are unavailable. Please check official channels for updates on reopening.

title: Beach safety closure in place from Buxton Village to near Ramp 43
description: The entire beachfront from the north end of the village of Buxton to near ORV ramp 43 is closed to public entry due to damages to dozens of houses and associated septic systems caused by coastal storm impacts.

title: What's Closed and What's Open on South Rim
description: Visit this link for a list of the available visitor services in Grand Canyon Village and at Desert View.

title: Beware of Harmful Algal Blooms
description: Harmful Algal Blooms with the presence of cyanotoxins have been found in multiple areas at Lake Powell in concentrations that are at the high end of safe exposure levels. Use caution and avoid unnecessary exposure to reservoir water if recreating.

title: Be City Smart: Take precautions to secure your vehicle and valuables.
description: This is an urban park. Don't hide valuables inside your car. Carry items with you on hike. Lock your vehicle and set car alarm. Report suspicious persons loitering/peeking in cars. Call 928-638-7805

title: Get Skyline Drive Status Updates
description: Sign up for Shenandoah Alerts by texting SHENALERTS to 888777. Any time there are closures, re-openings, or other important safety-related alerts, subscribers will receive a notification via text. You may unsubscribe at any time.

title: Big South Fork Announces Temporary Closure of Blue Heron Tipple Bridge
description: Big South Fork NRRA has announced the temporary closure of the historic Blue Heron Tipple Bridge, effective immediately in the interest of visitor safety. A recent bridge safety inspection conducted by the Federal Highway Administration identified structural issues specific to the pier’s bridge decking which is now leaning beyond acceptable standards.

title: Access to Big South Fork’s Scenic O&W Road Blocked Due to Bridge Failure
description: Road access to O&W Road was blocked by order of the State of Tennessee Department of Transportation. Pine Creek Bridge was closed by the state because it was deemed unsafe. The closed bridge is the first bridge on O&W Road after the Toomey Road intersection. The closed bridge is outside of the boundary of the Big South Fork National River & Recreation Area.

title: George Rogers Clark NHP in the Eastern Time Zone
description: George Rogers Clark National Historical Park is located in the Eastern Time Zone. The memorial closes 15 minutes before the visitor center.

title: Water Availability
description: There is NO water at the scorpion campgrounds and the trailhead for the cliff dwellings. Water is available at the Visitor Center, Woody's corral and TJ's corral.

title: Visitor Pass Needed to Visit George Washington Carver Museum
description: All visitors to Tuskegee University, including those visiting the George Washington Carver Museum located on campus, are required to obtain a visitor pass before entering. This policy is part of the university's safety and security procedures. Visitor passes are issued by Tuskegee University Public Safety. Stop by the Campus Public Safety building upon arrival to receive your pass at 1103 West Montgomery Road, Tuskegee, AL 36088.

title: New Policy on Fires and Lighting
description: Please be advised that a new policy is now in effect regarding the lighting and maintaining of fires within the park. To read the full details, including specific regulations and guidelines, please refer to the Superintendent's Compendium.

title: Limited Restroom Availability
description: Restrooms are available at the Independence Visitor Center at 6th & Market Streets, the corner of 5th and Chestnut Streets, and the Benjamin Franklin Museum (museum entrance requires a fee) in Franklin Court.

title: New Policy on Fires and Lighting
description: Please be advised that a new policy is now in effect regarding the lighting and maintaining of fires within the park. To read the full details, including specific regulations and guidelines, please refer to the Superintendent's Compendium.

title: Reduced cold-water flow at the Serpentine Hot Springs bathhouse
description: Cold water brought in via a pipe helps reduce the temperature of the water in the tub. With the reduced flow, temperatures in the tub are dangerously hot.

title: El Calderon Trail closed for construction August 11, 2025 through May 2026
description: The El Calderon Trail and parking area are closed from August 11, 2025, through late May 2026 for a major improvement project. NPS Road 100 will remain open for local traffic only, and the Continental Divide Trail will remain open for CDT thru-hikers.

title: Lighthouse Area Closed until June 30, 2026
description: The area around the Fort Washington Lighthouse is closed until June 30, 2026, at 11:59 p.m. as construction continues to rehabilitate the historic lighthouse.

title: Intermittent Visitor Center Phone Issues
description: The phone system at the Adams National Historical Park Visitor Center is currently experiencing intermittent audio issues.

title: Phone Outage
description: We are currently experiencing a disruption in our phone service at Lake Clark National Park and Preserve. Until further notice, please contact us via email for all inquiries and assistance. Email Contact: LACL_Information@nps.gov

title: Looking for a Grand Canyon view? A warning about mobile devices and map/navigation apps
description: Map apps may direct you to Parashant if you search for directions to "Grand Canyon" due the canyon's name in both the national park and monument names. The national park has paved roads and services. Parashant has no services and rugged/remote conditions.

title: Delays up to 1 hour are possible at Kelbaker Road Railroad Crossing near Kelso Depot
description: In the event there are train siding operations at the crossing near Kelso Depot, delays may be up to one hour. Please plan accordingly for this possibility with extra fuel and drinking water. Union Pacific Contact: 1 800 848-8715
relatedRoadEvents: [{'title': 'Kelso Railroad Crossing Delays 1 hour or more', 'id': '7F8876D0-161A-470B-8A06-854B512C8FE8', 'type': 'roadevent', 'url': 'https://www.nps.gov/moja/planyourvisit/conditions.htm'}]

title: Kelso Depot Visitor Center closed
description: Kelso Depot Visitor Center is closed for rehabilitation of the heating and cooling system. We expect to reopen the depot in 2026. The restrooms beside the depot are open.

title: Scale House Closed
description: The Scale House and Park Store are closed due to damage sustained during recent flooding.

title: Use caution on Cima Road
description: Use caution on Cima Road due to shoulder washouts.

title: Rough and damaged roads
description: Drive slowly and exercise due caution when driving in Mojave National Preserve. Rough and damaged roads exist on both paved and unpaved roads. Watch for potholes.

title: Lighting and Maintaining Fires Information
description: Fires are permitted only in designated areas and under specific conditions. A fire may be ignited and maintained only by using fuel sources designed and commonly used for warmth or the preparation of food, such as charcoal briquettes or natural firewood. Lighting or maintaining a fire with other materials including, but not limited to, flammable liquids, garbage, fireworks, plastics, aerosol canisters, batteries, or other manufactured or synthetic materials, is prohibited.

title: South Unit Address for Mobile Maps – Theodore Roosevelt NP
description: On all maps used on mobile devices, use the following address to reach the South Unit Visitor Center and Entrance Station 201 East River Road N. Medora, ND 58645

title: Trail Repairs at Franklin Canyon
description: Trail repairs will begin on Monday, September 29, on the Hastain Trail. Expect weekday closures Mon–Thurs on the southern switchbacks and eastern Discovery Trail. Trails remain open Fri–Sun. Park closes at sunset. Work will shift to Discovery Trail after Hastain is complete. Project is expected to last ~3 months, weather permitting.

title: Petrified Forest Trailhead Access Update: Use Approved Route
description: Directions to the Petrified Forest trailhead have changed. Please use the approved legal route from I-94, Exit 10 toward Camels Hump Lake and follow the clearly marked posts. This update helps maintain good cooperation with neighbors and respect park boundaries. For precise directions, use the link provided.

title: Dingmans Falls Trail, Road, and Access Closed for Bridge Replacement
description: Dingmans Falls site is closed for a bridge replacement project and trail rehabilitation. All traffic, including pedestrian, cyclist, and vehicle traffic, is prohibited on the road and trail during construction.

title: Launch Ramp Closures
description: Lake level changes will influence the functionality of all launch ramps. Find updated status of all launch ramps at the link.

title: Blue Basin Overlook Trail Closed
description: The 3.25 - mile Blue Basin Overlook trail in the Sheep Rock unit is closed until further notice. A mudslide and trail washouts have created unsafe conditions for hiking. The shorter 1.3- mile Island In Time trail remains open.

title: Trails Not Maintained During Winter
description: Trails at River Raisin National Battlefield Park are not maintained during winter months. Please use caution.

title: Trail Closure at Veterans Park at Balls Bluff (Leesburg, Virginia)
description: Veterans Park in Leesburg, Virginia, is closed for park improvements. All trails in the park are closed until Spring 2026.

title: Piñon Flats Campground, sites can be reserved 3 months in advance for 2026
description: Due to the need for improvements in Piñon Flats Campground, sites can be reserved 3 months in advance beginning in 2026.

title: Yellow Jacket (Vespula) Wasp Activity
description: Be aware of aggressive ground-nesting yellowjacket wasps. When threatened, leave the area. Do not swat wasps. If stung and you begin to experience serious symptoms or allergic reactions, call 911.

title: Attention Visitors, Cab drivers, Uber drivers, and transportation service drivers
description: DO NOT drop off your passengers at the visitor center. No trail access. Visitors must be driven to a trail system from the visitor center. If passengers want to go to the visitor center, WAIT FOR THEM in the parking lot until they come back out.

title: Road Construction Continues on a Section of Brotherton Road in Chickamauga Battlefield
description: The Brotherton Road closure, between Alexander's Bridge and LaFayette Roads, remains in effect as rehabilitation continues. During the closure, all public traffic is prohibited from using the closed section.
relatedRoadEvents: [{'title': 'Closure of a Section of Brotherton Road in Chickamauga Battlefield', 'id': '2447DCCC-35CB-487A-9E1D-ABD719B80885', 'type': 'roadevent', 'url': 'https://www.nps.gov/chch/planyourvisit/conditions.htm'}]

title: For Visitor information or assistance, Call (334) 727-3200
description: For visitor information or assistance at Tuskegee Institute National Historic Site, please call (334) 727-3200.For group reservations please call Sheryl Buchanan at (334) 421-3246.

title: Patriots Point Ferries Currently Wheelchair Inaccessible
description: Ferries to Fort Sumter from Patriots Point are not currently accessible for individuals who use mobility assistance devices or have trouble navigating stairs. Visitors with these needs should depart from the Fort Sumter Visitor Center at Liberty Square.

title: Park Store is Cashless
description: The Western National Parks store accepts credit/debit cards and contactless/mobile payments only.

title: Fire Restrictions Are In Effect Throughout Zion National Park
description: Fire Restrictions are in effect throughout Zion National Park.

title: Vehicle Length & Trailer Restrictions
description: No large vehicle turnaround after Faraway Ranch Parking (1 mile after entrance). Vehicles over 29 feet park at Faraway. Disconnect trailers at Faraway. Trailers with campground reservations proceed to camp. Scenic drive = 24 ft limit and no trailers.

title: No Pets are allowed on the Cliff Dwelling Trail
description: Pets are not allowed onto the Gila Cliff Dwelling Trail. Kennels are available at the Trailhead on a First Come First Serve basis and are available to those that are going on the Cliff Dwelling Trail not the Wilderness trails.

title: Fort Moultrie Fishing Dock Closure
description: The Fort Moultrie Fishing Dock is currently under construction through Spring 2026.

title: Pardon Our Dust: Chisos Basin Improvement Projects to Begin May 1, 2026
description: Work on two large construction projects in the Chisos Basin will begin in May 2026 and continue for approximately two years. Major disruptions in visitor access to the Chisos Basin are expected during this time. Big Bend National Park will remain OPEN.

title: For Visitor information or assistance, Call (334) 727-1247
description: For visitor information or assistance at Tuskegee Airmen National Historic Site, please call (334) 727-1247.

title: Park Store To Go Cashless
description: Starting August 14th, 2025 the park store will go cashless. All major credit cards, debit cards and mobile electronic payments will be accepted.

title: Technical Difficulties with Phones
description: Oregon Caves phone system is down. For general park info, please call the Illinois Valley Visitor Center at 541-592-5125. Thank you for your patience and understanding.

title: Limited Amphitheater Accessibility
description: Construction vehicles are operating through the parking lot & Lower Overlook Park - use caution & be vigilant for hazards. Lower Overlook & Amphitheater access is via stairs only for construction duration.

title: North Abutment Overlook Closure
description: The North Abutment deck leading to the overlook is in the process of being replaced. There is no visitor access to the North Abutment overlook until construction is complete.

title: Campground Closures
description: The following campgrounds are closed pending repairs: Pulltite (sites 1-13) and Dee Murray. Day use areas remain open for visitors.

title: Partial Reopening: Cross Estate Gardens
description: The Cross Estate gardens have reopened as of August 4. However, a portion of the trellis area remains closed due to storm damage and ongoing safety concerns. Please do not enter the roped-off section until repairs are complete.

title: WNP Bookstore is Cashless
description: The Western National Parks (WNP) bookstore has moved to a cashless operation at Timpanogos Cave National Monument. Purchases can be made with credit and debit cards as well as contactless payment methods including Google Pay, Apple Pay, and Samsung Pay.

title: William Johnson House Reopens!
description: The first floor exhibit hall of the William Johnson House is now open to the public daily from 8:30 am to 5:00 pm. Due to construction, the second floor family living quarters remain closed at this time.

title: Phone Lines Down
description: Chamizal National Memorial is experiencing temporary phone outages. We apologize for the inconvenience. Information regarding our hours of operation can be found under Plan Your Visit and questions can be directed to cham_information@nps.gov.

title: Tickets are required to enter Timpanogos Cave
description: Reservations are strongly recommended. Reservations are the only way to ensure a spot is available for your party. Please plan accordingly by visiting Recreation.gov to secure your tickets in advance. Availability of walk-up tickets is not guaranteed.

title: Extended Mt. Auburn Street and Sparks Street Closure
description: Local road closures and detours on Mount Auburn Street and Sparks Street, and a lane closure on Memorial Drive, may affect traffic and directions to the site. Please observe posted detour and traffic change signs.

title: Steel Creek Ranger Station Closed
description: The Steel Creek Ranger Station is closed. Please call Tyler Bend Visitor Center at 870-439-2502 for information about the park's Upper District.

title: Portion of the boardwalk is closed
description: The last section of boardwalk is closed for ADA accessibility. Sorry for the inconvenience.

title: Shenandoah National Park Moves to Fully Cashless Fee Collection July 1
description: On July 1, 2025, the park transitioned to a fully cashless system at our fee entrance stations. Only credit cards, debit cards, or mobile payments will be accepted.

title: Boardwalk Partially Closed for Construction
description: The lower section of the boardwalk loop is closed for construction at this time, and the Weston Lake Overlook is now open. Please click "more" below for a map highlighting the closed section and alternate routes.

title: No Recreational Vehicle (RV) Parking
description: Our parking lot cannot accommodate RVs or trailers. Check out our FAQs page or follow the link for a map to a nearby lot for parking and trailers to detach. RVs should NOT pull into the parking lot.

title: Phone lines working intermittently
description: Our phone lines are working intermittently. If you are not able to reach us by phone, please email us at bela_information@nps.gov.

title: Vehicle Entrance Reservation Not Needed
description: Although an entrance pass is required to enter Yellowstone National Park, vehicle reservations are not needed. You are encouraged to purchase an entrance pass before arriving, but you may also purchase passes at any entrance station upon arrival.

title: Sherrick Farm Trail Detour
description: There is a detour on the trail due to downed trees. Our staff is working diligently to remove these from the trail.

title: Charging Stations are Currently Unavailable
description: The charging stations at Skyland are currently out of service.

title: Use Caution with "Fossil Beds Information" Signs
description: The new visitor center is located at 17970 US Hwy 30, about one mile north of downtown Hagerman and the old visitor center. Highway signs are not up-to-date.

title: Don't Move a Mussel!
description: Hey Boaters, please clean, drain, and dry your vessel completely before setting out on Whiskeytown Lake. Thank you for doing your part to keep the Golden Mussel, an aquatic invasive species, out of the reservoir. Learn more about preventative measures.

title: Lakina River Update
description: A recently observed landslide across the Lakina River is creating a debris hazard on a popular packrafting route. The river water is cutting its way through, but is highly dynamic and should be expected to remain that way through the boating season.

title: Mount Locust Visitor Contact Station and Grounds Open Wednesday-Sunday
description: Mount Locust Visitor Contact Station is now open. The contact station and grounds are open Wednesday through Sunday. The historic inn/stand is closed indefinitely due to serious structural concerns. You can still view the inn/stand but cannot enter.

title: Belmont-Paul Women's Equality NM exterior lift
description: The exterior lift from Second Street to the entrance level is not fully functional. We apologize for the inconvenience. Contact us at bepa_info@nps.gov for more information.

title: Cashless Operations
description: Black Canyon's operations are now cashless. South Rim Campground is by reservation only via Recreation.gov. East Portal & North Rim campgrounds accept payment for first-come first-served sites ONLY using Scan & Pay through Recreation.gov's mobile app.

title: Auto-Theft Warning - Only Bring What You Need to the Park
description: You can prevent vehicle break-ins in parking lots and scenic overlooks. Before leaving your car for even a short period of time: 1) Remove all valuables, 2) Lock your doors. 3) Take your cell phones and electronics with you. Read article for more details.

title: Water Outage at Ponderosa Campground
description: The Ponderosa Campground is experiencing a temporary water outage. Please bring all the water you’ll need for your visit, including drinking water.

title: Possible Bridge Closure
description: The Circle Trail bridge under Winnewissa Falls may be closed temporarily due to flooding caused by spring rain. Please pay attention to signage posted in the Visitor Center and at the bridge. The rest of the park and Circle Trail remain open.

title: Contact Us
description: We continue to experience issues with our phone lines, please contact us via our email address: boha_information@nps.gov

title: Anchoring Permit Not Required
description: Anchoring is allowed without a permit in the designated location on the west side of Buck Island. Updated permitting procedures will be announced at a later date. Check back for updates.

title: Stehekin Flash Flood and Debris Flow Area
description: The Stehekin area is prone to flooding, which increases in a post-fire environment. Know before you go – visit the Post Fire Safety page for information on what to do in a flash flood and debris flow area

title: Major Road Closure in Corinth, MS
description: ALERT - Major Road Closure in Corinth, Mississippi. A major bridge replacement has begun on North Polk Street in Corinth, MS, and motorists will need to seek other routes. The work is not estimated to be completed until July or August 2026.

title: Possible Delays on Old Mine Road due to Van Campens bridge repairs
description: Due to a bridge repair project over the Van Campens Brook, visitors on Old Mine Road may encounter delays over Summer 2025. The road will remain open, with single-lane traffic during working hours.

title: Main Street Walpack Bridge Closed Until Further Notice
description: Main Street in Walpack Center is closed at the Flatbrook Bridge due to structural damage to the bridge.

title: Parking Lot Closure
description: Due to ongoing construction, the parking lot is closed. Ample street parking can be found along Dudley Street, including the Brookline Reservoir. For accessible parking, please visit our "Contact Us" page for more information.

title: Off Road Vehicle Inspections
description: ORV renewals & new permits will be issued at the Nathanial P. Reed Visitor Center Friday-Monday, 9am-1:30pm. No permits will be issued Tuesday-Thursday. Permits will be issued first-come first-served. Visit our ORV page for more information

title: Temporary Closure of Select Trails for Rehabilitation Project
description: The Old Guides Trail, Heritage Trail, Sunset Point Trail and areas of the Sinkhole Trail are currently closed due to a trail rehabilitation project.

title: If You Can't Reach Us By Phone, Please Email Us
description: Please call 208-933-4105 to talk to a park ranger. However, if the phone lines are down, contact a ranger through email at hafo_information@nps.gov

title: Construction
description: There is ongoing construction due to roofing project. Please practice caution on the grounds and do not enter marked areas.

title: Kīlauea eruption
description: Volcanic activity is episodic, with eruptions separated by pauses lasting several days or more. Eruption can be as short as a few hours. Check webcams and USGS volcano update for current conditions before visiting. Stay safe and follow all regulations.

title: Reminder: Look Before Parking
description: Visitors are reminded to park in marked parking spaces. Parking in the grass or other alternative areas is unauthorized. Please help us #KeepFortMonroeBeautiful.

title: Road Closure and Fishing Pier Impact
description: The Engineer Wharf, one of two fishing piers at Fort Monroe is temporarily closed. The Finger Pier remains open. Also closed is the section of Fenwick Road between Ingalls and the East Gate of the fort. Detour signs will aid travelers around the closure.

title: Roads May Be Icy - Please Use Caution!
description: Even though the sun is shining, the open roads in the park may still have icy patches, especially in the mornings and evenings when the temperatures drop. Please drive with caution.

title: Munising Falls Trail Closed Until Further Notice
description: The Munising Falls Trail within Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore is closed until further notice. Approximately 75-feet of the paved trail was washed out April 28th from recent heavy rainfall and seasonal snow melt.

title: Canyon from Toe of Exit Glacier to the Outwash Plain
description: The canyon from the toe of Exit Glacier to where Exit Creek opens into the Outwash Plain is susceptible to sudden outburst flood events. These events produce sudden surges of water, ice and rock. For your safety please stick to designated park trails.

title: For Current Visitor Information
description: For the most up to date information on Frances Perkins National Monument, please visit the website for the Frances Perkins Center at francesperkinscenter.org.

title: Butterfield Trail closed to horses and pack animals.
description: The trail remains open for hiking and foot traffic. For more information, see our policy page.

title: Pulltite Campground Partial Closure
description: Sites 1-13 remain closed. All other sites are open. Sites are available by reservation only - book online at https://www.recreation.gov/camping/campgrounds/234357

title: Fort Matanzas Only Accessible by Ferry Service
description: Fort Matanzas may only be accessed by our free ferry service. The boats operate Thursday through Monday; we do not operate the ferry on Tuesdays or Wednesdays. For more information about tickets and times, please visit the "Plan Your Visit" tab.

title: River Trail periodically closed under I-16
description: The section of the River Trail beneath Interstate 16 is closed during bridge construction when pedestrian safety is at risk and remains open when no significant hazards are present.

title: Ongoing Construction at the Emmett Till Interpretive Center
description: Due to ongoing construction, access to some areas of the Emmett Till Interpretive Center will be limited through May 5, 2025.

title: No potable water at Pinnacle
description: No potable water is available at the Pinnacle.

title: Diablo East Marina Cove, Scuba Cove, and Walkway Access Are Closed
description: The Diablo East Marina Cove, Scuba Cove, and walkway access are closed because of the presence of excessive docking material, unsafe walkway and dock, and unstable, loose ground. The area will reopen once these hazards have been corrected.

title: Fort Bowie Visitor Center Bookstore is now cashless!
description: For your convenience, we accept all major credit and debit cards, as well as mobile payment options. Electronic payment reduces transaction times, save money, and improves accountability. Thank you for your understanding and continued support!

title: Talisman-Barrett Beach Dock Closed Due to Ice Damage; Reduced Services for Summer Season
description: Due to damage from winter ice, the Talisman-Barrett Beach dock is closed to all public use until further notice. No lifeguards will be present during the summer season. Visitors should pack out trash and expect reduced restroom services.

title: North Union Avenue and Grant Circle Closed for Repairs
description: North Union Avenue and Grant Circle are closed to vehicular and pedestrian traffic for repairs. Entering these construction areas is prohibited. This work is to repair severe weather damage in 2020. The remainder of the park and tour road are open.

title: Ongoing work on Lower Town buildings
description: Facilities staff are doing repair work on several buildings in Lower Town. Please follow signs for alternate entrances and heed temporary closures.

title: Artifacts Removed From Museum
description: Due to leaks and humidity issues, most of the historic artifacts have been taken off exhibit to protect them until these issues have been resolved. Exhibit area is still open including text panels, maps, and hands-on-exhibits.

title: Secure trash and food
description: Pack it in, pack it out! There are no trash services in or around the monument. All food & trash must be secured inside a vehicle. Ravens routinely open zipped bags & tear into trash bags left outside or in the bed of a truck.

title: Park Operating Hours
description: Operating hours are 9:00 am to 3:30 pm Wednesday through Sunday until further notice.

title: South Unit temporarily closed south of Grand Teton Drive; Trailheads remain open.
description: The South Unit of Tule Springs Fossil Beds is temporarily closed to visitor access south of Grand Teton Drive, between Tule Springs Parkway and North Fifth Street in North Las Vegas for visitor safety during construction activities.

title: Melrose Mansion Guided Tours
description: Reservations for guided tours of the mansion must be made on Recreation.gov. The tour is $11.00 and are at 10, 11, 2, 3, and 4; masks are optional. The estate is open daily from 8:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. The grounds and outbuildings are free.

title: Hyde Street Pier Closure
description: Hyde Street Pier and access to historic ships will be closed to public until further notice starting 11/04/2024 in preparation for Hyde Street Pier renovation work.

title: Preservation Work Along Trails
description: Starting March 18, preservationists restoring the bronze state plaques along the entrance road, and the War of 1812 commemorative markers in the Orpheus Grove. Some of these areas may be temporarily closed while work is in progress.

title: Glover Archbold Trail detour
description: For visitor safety, the southern end of the Glover Archbold Trail is under an emergency closure until further notice due to aerial hazards. Please follow the posted detour along Foxhall Rd. / 44th St. NW / P St. NW.

title: For entrance fees, we accept digital passes and credit/debit cards
description: Digital passes or credit/debit cards are the ONLY method of payment at all fee collection areas. Pre-paid digital passes are available online and may be purchased in advance.

title: Hazardous Conditions on Trails
description: Visitors should exercise caution when hiking areas of the park that have opened since Hurricane Helene. Hazards on trails and in forested areas such as hanging tree limbs, debris on the ground, and washed-out sections of trails and bridges are possible.

title: Closures Due to Flood Damage
description: Some areas of the park including Walnut Canyon Desert Drive and some trails remain closed after extensive flood damage. The main park road to the visitor center and cavern is open.

title: Vehicle Break-Ins
description: Saguaro National Park is an urban park. Vehicle break-ins have occurred at trailheads around the park. Don't make yourself a target. Leave valuables at home, secure your vehicle, and report any suspicious activity.

title: Visitor Center Closed
description: The Salt River Bay NHP&EP Visitor Contact station remains closed due to damage caused by Hurricanes Irma/Maria. Reconstruction of the Visitor Contact Station continues.

title: Feral Dogs May Be Seen in Organ Pipe Cactus National Monument
description: Feral dogs may be present at the Twin Peaks Campground and around the Kris Eggle Visitor Center. Visitors should not attempt to feed or water these dogs or approach them in any way as they may carry disease or become hostile.

title: No Hunting Allowed in Castle Mountains National Monument
description: Hunting is illegal in Castle Mountains National Monument

title: Most Monument Roads Require Heavy Duty 4WD Vehicle to Travel Safely
description: Breaking down in the remote monument can be a life-threatening emergency. Most roads require a well-equipped, heavy-duty 4WD vehicle to travel. 2WD high clearance vehicles must use Walking Box Ranch Rd for entry and exit. No Visitor Facilities Available.

title: Hozomeen Gate Remains Closed
description: Until further notice, the gate at Hozomeen will remain closed; vehicle and foot traffic across the US/Canada border is prohibited. Visitors must enter through a designated port of entry.

title: List of trail closures post-Hurricane Helene
description: Many closures remain on the Trail following Hurricane Helene's devastation. This will remain true for the foreseeable future. Visit the Appalachian Trail Conservancy (ATC) website for an up-to-date list of closures. OBSERVE ALL CLOSURES. Stay safe!

title: Gas Pumps at Panamint Springs Resort are Closed at Night
description: Gasoline is available at Panamint Springs Resort from 7am-9:30pm, daily. Gas is available at Furnace Creek and Stovepipe Wells 24hrs.

title: Herbert Hoover NHS is Open. The Presidential Library and Museum is Closed for Renovations
description: The Herbert Hoover Presidential Library and Museum is closed to museum visitors with plans to reopen in 2026. The Research Room will remain open by appointment, and educational programming will continue during the renovation.

title: Reception Center Rehabilitation Project
description: The Eisenhower Reception Center is undergoing a rehabilitation construction project in 2025. This will not impact visitors' ability to tour the Eisenhower home. For more, click here.

title: America the Beautiful Passes Temporary Unavailable
description: At this time all sales of America the Beautiful Passes are temporary unavailable. Please check https://store.usgs.gov/recreational-passes for locations. We will advise when passes are available for sale. We apologize for any inconvenience.

title: Apertura retrasada hoy, 10 de enero debido a mal tiempo
description: Debido a condiciones del tiempo y riesgos elevados por la mañana, el parque abrirá en retraso a las 9 am el viernes, 10 de enero. Esperamos seguir las horas de atención regulares en el Centro Cultural, pero retrasos o cierres tempranos son posibles.

title: Delassus-Kern House and Grounds Closed
description: Due to hazardous conditions, the Delassus-Kern House and grounds are closed indefinitely.

title: Municipal Pier Permanent Closure
description: Municipal Pier is closed permanently. After a safety review with the U.S. Public Health Service, it was determined that the deterioration of Municipal Pier in Aquatic Park Cove is unsafe for public use.

title: Important Visitor Information – Screening Process in Effect
description: This is a new national park within an active military installation, which includes the current home of the US Army War College. There are no visitor services at this time. Please check back in the spring of 2025 for updates.

title: Ongoing Closure Notices
description: Devils Island dock and campsites, West Bay Club on Sand Island, and the trail on Stockton Island between Presque Isle Bay and Quarry Bay are closed. These closures affect only a small portion of the Lakeshore. Enjoy your visit.

title: Rip Currents and Rough Surf
description: Remember that rough surf is dangerous & rip currents can be deadly. Please use caution when swimming at Canaveral National Seashore.

title: Holiday Hours
description: On Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Eve, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day the visitor center will be closed (trails will remain open with access to restrooms on the north side of the building).

title: Please Email if Unable to Reach by Phone
description: Park phones are experiencing technical issues at this time. If unable to reach by phone, please feel free to email cong_information@nps.gov with any questions.

title: Part of Fort Totten Park remains closed for public safety
description: Out of an abundance of caution, Fort Totten Park will remain closed east of Fort Totten Drive, south of Gallatin Street/Metropolitan Branch Trail and north of Brookland Ave NE/Farragut St. due to the investigation involving two WWI-era munitions found.

title: Part of Fort Totten Park remains closed for public safety
description: Out of an abundance of caution, Fort Totten Park will remain closed east of Fort Totten Drive, south of Gallatin Street/Metropolitan Branch Trail and north of Brookland Ave NE/Farragut St. due to the investigation involving two WWI-era munitions found.

title: New Mexico Road Advisories
description: The New Mexico Department of Transportation updates its map of road advisories regularly. Visitors are encouraged to look for any possible road advisories along their route of travel beforehand.

title: Baltimore Area Traffic Advisory
description: Traffic in the Baltimore area has increased considerably in recent months due to the collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge which connected the Baltimore beltway. Please check the latest traffic conditions to plan your travel accordingly.

title: Trail Conditions
description: Rain may cause trails to be slick and overgrown. Staff and Volunteer Trail Crews clear trails regularly. Visitors should use caution and report trail issues to viis_interpretation@nps.gov

title: Baltimore Area Traffic Advisory
description: Traffic in the Baltimore area has increased considerably in recent months due to the collapse of the Francis Scott Key Bridge which connected the Baltimore beltway. Please check the latest traffic conditions to plan your travel accordingly.

title: Wupatki Pueblo Partial Closure
description: 3 rooms at Wupatki pueblo are closed due to an unstable wall. The trail is fully open.

title: Temporary Number Change
description: The park's phone number has temporarily changed. Please call 434-694-8904.

title: Help Prevent Aquatic Invasive Species (AIS) Infestation
description: A number of lakes are infested with an array of aquatic invasive species that damage natural ecosystems and waterways. Boaters are advised to Clean, Drain, and Dry all boats and equipment every time they leave the water.

title: Visitor Phone Line Intermittent
description: You may experience problems reaching park staff via the main visitor phone line which functions intermittently. You may email the park at lavo_information@nps.gov with general park questions.

title: Maurice Stephens Parking Lot Closed During Construction Project
description: The parking lot near the Maurice Stephens house, located on Rt 23 opposite from the Washington Memorial Chapel, is closed during construction and renovation work on the house. Visitors are encouraged to park in the Chapel lot for Grand Parade trail access

title: Migrant Notice
description: Like elsewhere in the Florida Keys, we have seen an increase in Cuban migrants arriving by boat from Cuba and landing on the islands of Dry Tortugas National Park. You may observe migrant landings at the park and visitor areas may be impacted.

title: Expect Crowding
description: Parking lots and popular destinations are crowded especially on weekends and days with warm weather between 10 am and 3 pm.

title: Current East Rim Trail Status
description: Click "more" to find out if the East Rim trails are open today. In good conditions the trails are open 6 am-11 pm for bikers and as posted for other trail users. In wet or hazardous conditions, the trail will close to all users to prevent damage.

title: Black Bears Frequenting the McAfee Knob Area in Virginia
description: Human-habituated bears have been frequently spotted in the McAfee Knob Area. Allowing bears access to human food or garbage causes it to lose its fear of humans. Follow all food storage regulations, use provided food lockers and never feed a bear.

title: Boardwalks at Watch Hill Partially Closed
description: Due to storm damage, partial closure of boardwalks at Watch Hill Nature Trail are in effect.

title: Partial Closure of Rocky Springs Trail
description: The Rocky Springs section is closed from the Rocky Springs campground at milepost 55 to the northern terminus at milepost 59 until further notice. Visitors should observe posted closures. The trail is unsafe due to trail erosion and deteriorated bridges.

title: Lasting Effects from 2021 Dixie Fire
description: Some facilities, infrastructure, and areas remain closed; please adhere to all closure signs. In re-opened areas, hazards like fallen trees, loose/falling rock, undefined trails, and hidden stump holes exist. Click more to learn about burned area safety.

title: Natchez Visitor Center Closed
description: The Natchez Visitor Center is closed for the renovation of the public lobby area. For those seeking visitor information, we direct you to VisitNatchez at the Depot on Boundary Street. Or their website, VisitNatchez.org. We apologize for any inconvenience.

title: Closure: Portion of the Awatixa Trail
description: The river bank section of trail below the Sakakawea Village and continuing upriver to the parking access with is currently closed due to ongoing erosion and project work.

title: Backcountry Camping Closed
description: Backcountry camping at the park is closed. There is no established opening date at this time.

title: Use Caution in and around Pedersen Lagoon
description: On August 7, 2024, a landslide triggered tsunami washed over portions of Pedersen Lagoon near Aialik Bay (59.8842N, -149.7309W). We recommend caution for those visiting the area in and around Pedersen Lagoon. Click more for additional information.

title: Bartlett Park Restrooms Closed
description: The restrooms at Bartlett Park are closed until further notice due to damage from a large fallen tree.

title: Centennial Trailhead Parking
description: Reminder: Overnight parking without a camping permit is prohibited. Permits are not issued specifically for parking. Shuttles are available in the Black Hills for through hikers on the Centennial Trail.

title: Interdune Boardwalk Closed
description: Due to the active safety corridor, the Interdune Boardwalk is closed. Please visit the park visitor center to learn about other options during your visit.

title: New Food Storage Guidelines to Reduce Human - Wildlife Interactions
description: In response to wolves accessing human food and garbage in and around Rock Harbor and campgrounds located on the east end of the island, new food storage guidelines to ensure the safety of visitors and wildlife are in effect.

title: Rehabilitation of Colonial Parkway continues with additional Parkway closures
description: Beginning Tuesday, April 9, 2024, construction will close the Colonial Parkway to all travel in either direction between Yorktown and VA Route 199. Follow all detour signs. See additional closure information in the link below.

title: Rehabilitation of Colonial Parkway continues with additional Parkway closures
description: Beginning Tuesday, April 9, 2024, construction will close the Colonial Parkway to all travel in either direction between Yorktown and VA Route 199. Follow all detour signs. See additional closure information in the link below.

title: Park Passes
description: Park passes, including: America the Beautiful, Veteran, Lifetime, etc., are UNAVAILABLE for purchase/pickup within Big Cypress. Shark Valley Visitor Center (Everglades) is the closest location to Big Cypress for purchase.

title: Canine distemper is believed to be present in the park.
description: Contact with saliva from wild animals can transmit distemper to pets, including from but not limited to water bowls, food bowls, trash etc. Distemper is not a threat to humans but can be deadly to pets. Ensure your pets are vaccinated or keep them home.

title: Hovenweep has gone cashless
description: The park now accepts only mobile or electronic payments for entrance & camping fees. You can reserve campsites and buy entrance passes on Recreation.gov, or only use debit/credit cards to pay in person.

title: Natural Bridges has gone cashless
description: The park now accepts only mobile or electronic payments for entrance & camping fees. You can reserve campsites and buy entrance passes on Recreation.gov, or only use debit/credit cards to pay in person.

title: Sick Birds and Wildlife, H5N1
description: Be cautious around sea lions, and dead or sick birds. If you get “bombed” by a bird, wash with soap and water immediately, monitor for signs of illness, and call your doctor. Report beached, sick or distressed wildlife to Wildlife Mortality Reporting.

title: Bear Alert
description: Black bears inhabit many of the Apostle Islands. Follow safety guidelines: store food and scented items properly, hike in groups, stay attentive, and make noises if you encounter a bear in a visitor area.

title: Partial Closures of Calumet Bike Trail
description: Due to recent re-construction efforts, partial closures of the Calumet Bike Trail will be in effect through this fall and sometime next year. Please use caution if riding on the trail (see below) and off the trail (because of the closures).

title: Private Inholdings: Sand & Rocky Islands
